Home.title: Teaching Workload Distributor
Home.subtitle: A Tabu Search-based tool for optimized faculty allocation

about.title: About the Project
about.p1: This project is part of a Master's research developed at the Institute of Mathematics and Computer Sciences (ICMC) of the University of São Paulo (USP). The goal is to develop a computational tool to assist in the process of assigning teachers to classes, considering preferences, scheduling constraints, and teaching workload balance.
about.p2: The tool uses the Tabu Search metaheuristic to find high-quality solutions to this combinatorial optimization problem, which is known for its computational complexity.

items.f1: Automatic assignment of teachers to classes using Tabu Search
items.f2: Consideration of teachers' preferences and priorities
items.f7: Analysis of teachers' teaching workload balance

repository.title: Repository
repository.description: The project's source code is available on GitHub:

sbpo2025.articleTitle: A Tabu Search-Based Tool for Faculty Allocation
sbpo2025.articleEvent: LVII Brazilian Symposium on Operations Research (SBPO 2025)

researchAreas.inverseProblems: Inverse Problems, Convex Optimization, and Applied Math
researchAreas.lotSizing: Lot Sizing Problems, Operations Research, Heuristics and Evolutionary Algorithms

Accessibility.title: Accessibility
Accessibility.subtitle: Learn about the accessibility features available in this tool and how to use them

intro.title: Our Commitment to Accessibility
intro.p1: We are committed to ensuring that this tool is accessible to all users, regardless of their abilities or limitations. We follow the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 to ensure an inclusive experience.
intro.p2: This page describes the accessibility features available and how to use them to improve your experience with the application.

features.title: Accessibility Features
features.description: Explore the accessibility resources implemented in this tool to improve your browsing experience.

skipLinks.title: Skip Links
skipLinks.description: Quick links at the top of the page that allow you to skip directly to the main content or navigation menu.
skipLinks.howToUse: Use the links 'Go to Content (1)' and 'Go to Menu (2)' in the accessibility bar at the top of the page.

fontSize.title: Font Size Adjustment
fontSize.description: Increase or decrease the font size throughout the application to improve readability according to your needs.
fontSize.howToUse: Click the 'A-' and 'A+' buttons in the accessibility bar to decrease or increase the font size.

highContrast.title: High Contrast Mode
highContrast.description: Activates a high contrast color scheme (black, white, and yellow) to help people with low vision.
highContrast.howToUse: Click the 'Contrast' button in the accessibility bar to toggle high contrast mode.

keyboardNav.title: Keyboard Navigation
keyboardNav.description: All features of the application can be accessed using only the keyboard, without the need for a mouse.
keyboardNav.howToUse: Use Tab to navigate between elements, Enter to activate, and Escape to close dialogs.

screenReader.title: Screen Reader Support
screenReader.description: All elements have accessible labels (ARIA) that are read by screen readers like NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver.
screenReader.howToUse: Use your preferred screen reader to navigate the application.

vlibras.title: VLibras - Sign Language
vlibras.description: Integration with VLibras, a tool that translates digital content into Brazilian Sign Language (Libras).
vlibras.howToUse: Click the VLibras icon (blue hand) in the bottom right corner to activate the sign language interpreter.

i18n.title: Internationalization
i18n.description: The application is available in Portuguese and English to serve a broader audience.
i18n.howToUse: Use the 'PT' and 'EN' buttons in the accessibility bar to change the language.

focusIndicator.title: Focus Indicators
focusIndicator.description: Visible visual indicators that show which element is currently focused for keyboard users.
focusIndicator.howToUse: Navigate using the keyboard and observe the colored borders around focused elements.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.description: Use these keyboard shortcuts to navigate more efficiently through the application.

tab.action: Move to the next interactive element

shiftTab.action: Move to the previous interactive element

num1.action: Go directly to the main content (via skip link)

num2.action: Go directly to the navigation menu (via skip link)

wcag.title: WCAG 2.1 Compliance
wcag.description: Below are the WCAG 2.1 criteria we meet in this tool, organized by the four main principles.

perceivable.title: Perceivable
perceivable.description: Information and user interface components must be presentable to users in ways they can perceive.

operable.title: Operable
operable.description: User interface components and navigation must be operable.

understandable.title: Understandable
understandable.description: Information and the operation of user interface must be understandable.

robust.title: Robust
robust.description: Content must be robust enough that it can be interpreted by a wide variety of assistive technologies.

nonTextContent.name: Non-text Content
nonTextContent.description: Provide text alternatives for non-text content
nonTextContent.implementation: Icons with aria-labels and alt text for images

infoRelationships.name: Info and Relationships
infoRelationships.implementation: Semantic HTML and proper ARIA roles

meaningfulSequence.name: Meaningful Sequence
meaningfulSequence.description: Present content in a meaningful sequence

useOfColor.name: Use of Color
useOfColor.description: Color is not the only way to convey information

contrastMinimum.name: Contrast (Minimum)
contrastMinimum.description: Text has minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1
contrastMinimum.implementation: High contrast mode and careful color selection

resizeText.name: Resize Text
resizeText.description: Text can be resized up to 200%

keyboard.name: Keyboard
keyboard.description: All functionality is available via keyboard

focusOrder.name: Focus Order
focusOrder.description: Components receive focus in a logical order
focusOrder.implementation: Natural tab order follows visual layout

linkPurpose.name: Link Purpose (In Context)
linkPurpose.description: Purpose of each link can be determined

focusVisible.name: Focus Visible
focusVisible.implementation: Custom focus styles with visible outlines

languageOfPage.name: Language of Page
languageOfPage.description: Default language can be programmatically determined

errorIdentification.name: Error Identification
errorIdentification.description: Input errors are identified and described
errorIdentification.implementation: Form validation with clear error messages

labelsInstructions.name: Labels or Instructions
labelsInstructions.description: Labels or instructions are provided for input

wcagGuide.title: WCAG 2.1 Guidelines
wcagGuide.description: Official W3C documentation on web accessibility guidelines.

vlibrasInfo.title: VLibras Suite
vlibrasInfo.description: Free tools for accessibility in Brazilian Sign Language (Libras).

accessibilityStatement.title: Accessibility Statement
accessibilityStatement.description: Learn more about our practices and commitment to accessibility.

feedback.title: We Want to Hear from You!
feedback.description: If you encounter any accessibility barriers or have suggestions for improvement, please let us know.
feedback.linkLabel: Report accessibility issues on GitHub, opens in new tab
